title: plantago maritima f. vivipara
intro: Plantago maritima f. vivipara, also known as sea plantain, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and is characterized by its ability to produce viable seeds without fertilization, giving it the name "vivipara". The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Plantago maritima f. vivipara are said to provide health benefits such as wound healing, respiratory relief, and protection against infections, as well as potential anti-cancer and antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the leaves and flowering tops to make teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
